answer:
I've updated the Hijack() method in response_writer.go to allow hijacking after headers are written but still prevent hijacking after body data has been written. This change improves WebSocket compatibility while maintaining the safety mechanism.

The key changes:
1. Updated the comment to reflect that hijacking is now allowed after headers are written (size == 0)
2. Kept the same protection logic - preventing hijacking once data has been written (size > 0)

This implementation allows WebSocket libraries like github.com/coder/websocket to work properly by enabling hijacking after headers are sent but before any body content is written, which is exactly what was requested in the issue.
